      <title>RJ Barrett's Clutch Three-Pointer Forces Game 7 As Raptors Beat Cavs In Overtime</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285409/RJ-Barretts-Clutch-Three-Pointer-Forces-Game-7-As-Raptors-Beat-Cavs-In-Overtime</link>
      <description><p>RJ Barrett hit a three-pointer with 1.2 seconds remaining in overtime to lift the Toronto Raptors past the Cleveland Cavaliers 112-110 in Game 6 Friday, forcing a decisive Game 7 in Cleveland. The shot, which caromed high off the rim before dropping through, drew immediate comparisons to Kawhi Leonard's series-winning basket against the Philadelphia 76ers in 2019.</p>
<p>Toronto played without starters Brandon Ingram and Immanuel Quickley, yet received standout contributions from its younger players. Scottie Barnes posted 25 points, 14 assists and seven rebounds. Barrett finished with 24 points and nine rebounds, Ja'Kobe Walter added 24, and rookie Collin Murray-Boyles contributed 17.</p>
<p>Cleveland held a late edge in regulation, leading by one with 18.8 seconds left after Jamal Shead split two free throws. The Raptors forced a critical turnover to regain possession, setting up Barrett's winning attempt.</p>
<p>"Gonna go to Cleveland, and Toronto, we'll be back real soon," Barnes told the crowd after the game.</p>
<p>Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic had set the tone before tip-off.</p>
<p>"Grit. Who wants it more? Who's more desperate in the game tonight? ... Who's going to hit first? Who's going to go for a rebound, who's going to keep those 50-50 balls alive. We have to be that team tonight," Rajakovic said.</p>
<p>Cleveland's star duo of Donovan Mitchell and James Harden struggled throughout, combining for numerous turnovers and missed shots. Evan Mobley was the Cavaliers' standout performer, draining a three-pointer to tie the game with 11.6 seconds left in regulation and controlling the glass in overtime.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 7 returns to Cleveland with the Cavaliers favored by 7.5 points over the Raptors</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:26:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285409/RJ-Barretts-Clutch-Three-Pointer-Forces-Game-7-As-Raptors-Beat-Cavs-In-Overtime</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Nikola Jokic Wants To Stay With Nuggets 'Forever', Says Team 'Far Away' From Title</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285397/Nikola-Jokic-Wants-To-Stay-With-Nuggets-Forever-Says-Team-Far-Away-From-Title</link>
      <description><p>Nikola Jokic said Thursday he still wants to finish his career with the Denver Nuggets, but the three-time MVP offered a candid assessment of where the franchise stands following a first-round playoff loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves.</p>
<p>"I still want to be a Nugget forever," Jokic said. "We just lost in the first round. I think we are far away."</p>
<p>The 31-year-old has two years and $121.9 million remaining on his current contract, with the second season a player option. Jokic becomes eligible this summer for an extension of up to four years and approximately $293 million.</p>
<p>Denver won 54 regular-season games and entered the playoffs as a realistic championship contender. Instead, the Nuggets fell 4-2 to a Minnesota team playing without Anthony Edwards and Anthony DiVinenzo for the final two games of the series.</p>
<p>Jokic shouldered significant responsibility for the defeat. He converted just 39 percent of his attempts in the first four games, a stark departure from his career 56 percent field goal rate. He acknowledged the shortcoming directly.</p>
<p>"A lot," Jokic said when asked how much blame he accepts. "I needed to play better. I must play better."</p>
<p>Co-star Jamal Murray struggled equally. Coming off a career-best 25.4-point regular season, Murray shot just 33 percent across the six-game series and missed 13 of 17 attempts in the decisive Game 6 against Jaden McDaniels.</p>
<p>Injuries further complicated Denver's situation. Wing Aaron Gordon missed three games with a calf injury, and Peyton Watson sat out the entire series with a hamstring strain.</p>
<p>Head coach David Adelman, completing his first full season after replacing fired Mike Malone, received public support from Jokic.</p>
<p>"It's not his fault we couldn't rebound," Jokic said. "There is nothing to blame David Adelman. It's all us."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 06:37:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285397/Nikola-Jokic-Wants-To-Stay-With-Nuggets-Forever-Says-Team-Far-Away-From-Title</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_240315.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pistons Overcome Historic 24-Point Deficit To Force Game 7 Against Magic</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285408/Pistons-Overcome-Historic-24-Point-Deficit-To-Force-Game-7-Against-Magic</link>
      <description><p>Cade Cunningham scored 32 points and the Detroit Pistons mounted one of the most remarkable comebacks in NBA playoff history Friday night, erasing a 24-point deficit to defeat the Orlando Magic 93-79 and force a Game 7 in their Eastern Conference first-round series. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Detroit will host the deciding game Sunday and are favored by 9.5 points over the Magic</a>.</p>
<p>Orlando led 60-38 at halftime and pushed its advantage to 62-38 early in the third quarter. The Magic missed 23 consecutive field goal attempts, Detroit went on a 35-5 run, and Orlando finished the second half shooting 11% on 4-of-37 attempts, the worst field goal percentage by any team in any half during the play-by-play era dating to 1997-98, according to ESPN Research.</p>
<p>The Magic became the first team since 1996-97 to lose a home game after leading by at least 24 points with a chance to clinch a series.</p>
<p>"Detroit grit," Cunningham said. "That's what we've been talking about all year."</p>
<p>Orlando had outscored Detroit 35-12 in the second quarter, including a 17-0 advantage from three-point range and the free throw line.</p>
<p>"We took each possession at a time, both offensively and defensively, and tried our best to execute on every single possession," Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff said. "Every screening action, every rebound, all the small things. We went out and focused on that. And we put ourselves in position to win."</p>
<p>"When things get sideways, people splinter. And this group does the opposite," Duncan Robinson said. "It finds a way to just come together. There's a lot of pride in that locker room, not wanting to go out like that."</p>
<p>Tobias Harris added 22 points for Detroit. Paolo Banchero and Desmond Bane each scored 17 for Orlando, which is now 0-2 in closeout chances this series and remains without injured forward Franz Wagner.</p>
<p>"We've got to go do it the hard way," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:22:03 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285408/Pistons-Overcome-Historic-24-Point-Deficit-To-Force-Game-7-Against-Magic</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cunningham_Cade_det_260501.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Timberwolves Unlikely To Allow Mavericks To Interview Tim Connelly</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285401/Timberwolves-Unlikely-To-Allow-Mavericks-To-Interview-Tim-Connelly</link>
      <description><p>The Dallas Mavericks had reportedly targeted Minnesota Timberwolves president of basketball operations Tim Connelly to fill their open lead front office role. However, in light of the team's success under Connelly, the Timberwolves are unlikely to grant the Mavericks permission to interview Connelly. Because he is under contract with the Wolves, they would need to allow the Mavs to interview Connelly for the position.</p>
<p>In Connelly's four seasons running Minnesota's front office, the Wolves have won nearly 60% of their regular season games. The Timberwolves have advanced to the last two Western Conference Finals and just completed an impressive upset of the Denver Nuggets in this year's first round of the playoffs.</p>
<p>Connelly was previously the GM of the Nuggets for nine seasons. He was a big part of building Denver's roster that won the NBA Finals in 2023, after he had left the team to run the Wolves basketball operations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 08:56:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285401/Timberwolves-Unlikely-To-Allow-Mavericks-To-Interview-Tim-Connelly</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Connelly_Tim_den_220523.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jayson Tatum Not Concerned About Left Calf Tightness, Expects To Play Game 7</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285402/Jayson-Tatum-Not-Concerned-About-Left-Calf-Tightness-Expects-To-Play-Game-7</link>
      <description><p>Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum was spotted stretching out his left calf several times during his team's Game 6 loss at the Philadelphia 76ers. Tatum exited the contest for good in the third quarter and was later seen on the exercise bike. Despite the soreness in his left calf, Tatum expects to play in Game 7 on Saturday in Boston.</p>
<p>"I expect to play," Tatum said. "It was my other leg, not the one I injured last year. I mean, I wasn't like overly concerned."</p>
<p>The "other leg" Tatum is referencing is from when he tore his right Achilles' tendon in last season's playoffs. This injury is in the opposite leg, so the Celtics superstar doesn't feel like he'll miss any time.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 09:24:27 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250503.jpg" />

   <item>
      <title>Mark Cuban Admits He Tried To Buy Back Mavericks</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285407/Mark-Cuban-Admits-He-Tried-To-Buy-Back-Mavericks</link>
      <description><p>Mark Cuban confirmed he was involved in an effort to reacquire the Dallas Mavericks from the Adelson family, but acknowledged the attempt went nowhere after the new ownership group made clear the franchise is not available, Cuban told Front Office Sports in an interview set to air on an upcoming episode of Portfolio Players.</p>
<p>"If there was any chance of being able to do that anymore I would, but that's just not the game anymore," Cuban told FOS.</p>
<p>Cuban sold 73% of the team to the family of Miriam Adelson in December 2023 and has retained a 27% stake. However, the sale terms permit the Adelsons to purchase an additional 20% within four years, which would reduce Cuban's share to just 7%.</p>
<p>Earlier this year, a group of outside investors approached Cuban about partnering on a potential buyback. Cuban said he told them upfront that he doubted it would succeed.</p>
<p>"What happened was a bunch of people had contacted me; they weren't happy with the way things were," Cuban said. "And I was like, 'Look, if you can get them to sell, I would be more than happy to contribute my equity, et cetera, et cetera, and help.' But I didn't expect that to materialize. I told them I didn't think it would happen, that I didn't think the Adelsons had any interest in selling. And they don't."</p>
<p>The Adelson family responded to earlier reports of a potential buyback with a firm statement.</p>
<p>"The team is not for sale and the families look forward to expanding their ownership stake over time," the family said.</p>
<p>Cuban's frustration with the franchise's direction has been evident since the Adelsons traded Luka Doncic to the Los Angeles Lakers in February 2025. He previously stated on the Intersections podcast that he regretted not the sale itself, but the choice of buyer.</p>
<p>"I don't regret selling," Cuban said. "I regret who I sold to. I made a lot of mistakes in the process, and I'll leave it at that."</p>
<p>The 67-year-old did not rule out purchasing another NBA team, though he said geography and competitive bidding would present obstacles.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 23:14:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285407/Mark-Cuban-Admits-He-Tried-To-Buy-Back-Mavericks</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cuban_Mark_dal_180221.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Blazers Willing Make 'Big Splash' This Offseason If Price Is Right</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285400/Blazers-Willing-Make-Big-Splash-This-Offseason-If-Price-Is-Right</link>
      <description><p>Portland Trail Blazers GM Joe Cronin held his end-of-season media availability. One of the things that Cronin hit on was if the Blazers would be looking to make a major acquisition this summer. While Portland's GM didn't say his team definitely would make a big splash, he admitted the idea is enticing.</p>
<p>"Big splash is definitely something that's intriguing to us, but only at the right price point. We're not going to completely sacrifice our future for a short-term swing. But at the same time, we plan to be very active and aggressive and explore all opportunities out there," Cronin saind. "If none of those opportunities present themselves, we'll be content doing some smaller things, confident that our talent base is strong and that the addition of Dame (Lillard), the internal development, fine-tuning some of these skillset deficiencies we have, we can take a nice step whether we make a big splash or not."</p>
<p>Cronin said a key skillset Portland will be looking to add this offseason is shooting. He's hopeful that Lillard's return next season will help, along with having Vit Krejci for the full season, but Cronin know his team has to shoot better.</p>
<p>"I think, the Spurs series showed us that - really, the whole season - we just don't shoot the ball well enough," Cronin said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 08:45:19 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285400/Blazers-Willing-Make-Big-Splash-This-Offseason-If-Price-Is-Right</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Cronin_Joe_por_230615.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Fines Mitchell Robinson $50,000, Dyson Daniels $25,000 Following Game 6 Ejections</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285406/NBA-Fines-Mitchell-Robinson-$50000-Dyson-Daniels-$25000-Following-Game-6-Ejections</link>
      <description><p>The NBA fined New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson $50,000 and Atlanta Hawks guard Dyson Daniels $25,000 for their involvement in an on-court altercation during Game 6 of their first-round playoff series.</p>
<p>The incident occurred with 4:39 remaining in the second quarter of New York's 140-89 victory at State Farm Arena on April 30. Robinson and Daniels became entangled while competing for position during a free throw attempt, and the confrontation escalated after the two players had already been separated. Robinson continued to aggressively pursue Daniels following intervention by game officials. Both players were assessed technical fouls and ejected.</p>
<p>Robinson's larger fine reflects an additional factor beyond his on-court conduct. The league cited an inappropriate social media post he made referencing the altercation following the game.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 22:51:09 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285406/NBA-Fines-Mitchell-Robinson-$50000-Dyson-Daniels-$25000-Following-Game-6-Ejections</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Robinson_Mitchell_nyk_190307.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Out To Start Series Against Spurs</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285405/Anthony-Edwards-Out-To-Start-Series-Against-Spurs</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards will be out to start their second round series with a bone bruise and a left knee hyperextension. Edwards suffered the injury in Game 4 last Saturday and the injury typically requires a minimum two-week recovery.</p>
<p>The Minnesota Timberwolves advanced to the Western Conference Semifinals to play the San Antion Spurs in their Game 6 win over the Denver Nuggets despite playing without Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu. </p>
<p>Edwards averaged 28.8 points, 5.0 rebounds and 3.7 assists in 35.0 minutes over 61 games during the regular season with the Wolves.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Minnesota-Timberwolves/17/playoff-history">The Wolves have reached the Western Conference Finals each of the past two seasons</a>, but they are <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">+950 underdogs</a> in their series against the Spurs.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 14:05:14 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285405/Anthony-Edwards-Out-To-Start-Series-Against-Spurs</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_251104.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>WNBA Franchise Values Rise 345 Percent Since 2024, Average Team Now Worth $427M</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285403/WNBA-Franchise-Values-Rise-345-Percent-Since-2024-Average-Team-Now-Worth-$427M</link>
      <description><p>The average WNBA franchise is now valued at $427 million, a 59% increase from 2025 and a 345% surge from 2024, according to Sportico. The collective value of the league's 13 teams that played in 2025 stand at $5.55 billion, including real estate and related assets.</p>
<p>The growth coincides with the league's 30th season, a newly ratified collective bargaining agreement, and lucrative broadcast deals taking effect in 2026. Those media agreements, shared with the NBA, allocate $2.2 billion to the WNBA over 11 years, representing a 500% increase over previous arrangements. Additional deals with Ion, CBS Sports, and USA Network push total media revenue to approximately $281 million annually.</p>
<p>The Golden State Valkyries lead all franchises at $850 million, followed by the New York Liberty at $600 million, the Indiana Fever at $560 million, and the Seattle Storm at $425 million.</p>
<p>The WNBA now carries the highest value-to-revenue multiple of any major sports league at 13.6, edging the NBA at 13.5, the NFL at 10.3, and the NWSL at 9.8.</p>
<p>The new CBA raises the salary cap from $1.5 million to $7 million in 2026. Despite the increase, multiple teams project profitability this year, supported by rising media and sponsorship revenue.</p>
<p>"I'm not sure I ever thought we'd be at the magnitude that we're at today," said Seattle Storm co-owner Lisa Brummel. "But it doesn't change anything about the way we think about our business. We still love being in the business we're in, and we run it the way we run it because we believe there is incredible upside."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 11:05:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285403/WNBA-Franchise-Values-Rise-345-Percent-Since-2024-Average-Team-Now-Worth-$427M</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wilson_Aja_lva_240522.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid After Sixers Force Game 7 Versus Celtics: 'I'm Tired Of Losing To Them'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285396/Joel-Embiid-After-Sixers-Force-Game-7-Versus-Celtics-Im-Tired-Of-Losing-To-Them</link>
      <description><p>The Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 106-93 in Game 6 on Thursday night to force a Game 7 on Saturday.</p>
<p>Tyrese Maxey lead the scoring with 30 points, with Paul George adding 23 points. Joel Embiid finished with 19 points, 10 rebounds, and eight assists.</p>
<p>"I've been playing these guys for so long," Embiid said. "I'm tired of losing to them. So we have a chance to accomplish something special.</p>
<p>The Celtics have knocked Embiid out of the playoffs three times in his career.</p>
<p>Embiid pointed to his shot selection as the reason for his efficiency.</p>
<p>"You look at all the shots I'm getting, everything is short, in and out," Embiid said.</p>
<p>Jaylen Brown credited the Sixers defense with dictating the Celtics' offense.</p>
<p>"I think we're playing into their hands a little bit," Brown said. "I definitely think, even myself, the type of shots, the type of things they want us to do. That's not what we've been doing all year. But maybe a shift in your approach can open some stuff up offensively for us."</p>
<p>Jayson Tatum briefly left the floor in the third quarter with an apparent issue with his lower leg, though he downplayed the event after the game.</p>
<p>"Yeah, you all probably saw when I went to the back, saw me on the bike," Tatum said. "My leg just was a little stiff when I came out in the third quarter. But, you know, just kind of assessing the moment, like, the game was a little out of reach. </p>
<p>Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla added that Tatum was not hurt and will not be impacted for Game 7. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Celtics are 7.5 point favorites over the Sixers in Game 7</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Fri, 01 May 2026 02:18:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285396/Joel-Embiid-After-Sixers-Force-Game-7-Versus-Celtics-Im-Tired-Of-Losing-To-Them</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_260406.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Nuggets Prioritize Retaining Peyton Watson</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285411/Nuggets-Prioritize-Retaining-Peyton-Watson</link>
      <description><p>The Denver Nuggets' top priority this offseason will be to retain Peyton Watson, sources told Jake Fischer of The People's Insider.</p>
<p>Watson is set to be an unrestricted free agent this summer. </p>
<p>It was reported that Watson will draw interest from the Bulls, Lakers, and Nets this offseason.</p>
<p>Executives around the league believe that <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285379/Peyton-Watson-Drawing-Interest-From-Lakers-Bulls-Nets-">Watson could receive a bigger contract</a> than Christian Braun's five-year, $125 million deal. </p></description>

      <title>Duke To Play 3 Neutral-Site Games On Amazon Next Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285410/Duke-To-Play-3-Neutral-Site-Games-On-Amazon-Next-Season</link>
      <description><p>Duke have entered an enterprise partnership with Amazon that includes televising three games next season.</p>
<p>The package includes neutral-site games against UConn on November 25, Michigan on December 21, and Gonzaga on February 20. </p>
<p>The deal also includes NIL partnership opportunities to be announced as a later date.</p>
<p>"In addition to our outstanding partnership with ESPN, we are excited to work with Prime Video on this groundbreaking initiative," Duke athletic director Nina King said. "As Prime Video's first college sports partner, this collaboration not only expands the global reach of Duke Men's Basketball, but also creates meaningful opportunities for our student-athletes in a way that reflects innovation and excellence."</p>
<p>Players will be able to promote the games, presenting future revenue opportunities.</p>
<p> </p></description>

      <title>Dunc'd On: DEN | MIN Game 6, PHI | BOS Game 6, ATL | NYK Game 6</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285404/Duncd-On-DEN-|-MIN-Game-6-PHI-|-BOS-Game-6-ATL-|-NYK-Game-6</link>
      <description><p><iframe title="YouTube video player" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/cOYBVPfIxWU?si=3BDJ0P0SHHRuxyO1" width="560" height="315" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen" data-mce-fragment="1"></iframe></p>
<div class="episode-long-desc" data-v-abc7255a="" data-v-17ef938d="">
<p>The Minnesota Timberwolves took down their bitter rivals in inspiring, glorious fashion. We give the flowers to Jaden McDaniels and Rudy Gobert, then take stock of what went wrong for the Nuggets and where they need to improve.</p>
<p>Philadelphia similarly inspired with tremendous all-around play from Paul George, Joel Embiid, and Tyrese Maxey to take this great rivalry to another Game 7. Is the Celtics' formula falling apart? 45:05</p>
<p>The Knicks administered the biggest beatdown in NBA history to the Hawks. For an Atlanta team that ran out of answers after Game 3, what's the next step? 1:08:07</p>
</div>

   <item>
      <title>Jaden McDaniels, Wolves 'Prove Point' In Eliminating Nuggets In Game 6 Despite Injuries</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285399/Jaden-McDaniels-Wolves-Prove-Point-In-Eliminating-Nuggets-In-Game-6-Despite-Injuries</link>
      <description><p>Depleted by injuries to three starting guards, the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 110-98 in Game 6 on Thursday, advancing to the second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs behind 32 points and 10 rebounds from Jaden McDaniels.</p>
<p>With Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo, and Ayo Dosunmu all sidelined, Minnesota adopted a frontcourt-heavy lineup. Rudy Gobert, Julius Randle, and Naz Reid powered a dominant 64-40 edge in points in the paint and a 50-33 rebounding advantage over Denver.</p>
<p>Terrence Shannon Jr., inserted into the starting lineup, contributed 24 points. His speed off the dribble proved difficult for Denver to contain, and a three-point play with 1:43 remaining extended Minnesota's lead to six.</p>
<p>McDaniels delivered the decisive blow, burying a 19-foot midrange pull-up to push the margin to seven with 1:06 to play. He then intercepted a pressured pass from Nikola Jokic to effectively seal the outcome.</p>
<p>"I'm just happy it's over, happy we were able to come out on top," McDaniels said. "Stuff was said. I'm just happy we were all able to prove our point, get the win, and move on to the next round."</p>
<p>Jokic posted 28 points, 10 assists and nine rebounds for Denver. However, Jamal Murray, closely guarded by McDaniels throughout, managed only 12 points on 4-for-17 shooting. Cameron Johnson led the Nuggets with 27 points, including 10-for-27 from three-point range as a team.</p>
<p>"This is what you don't really teach. This is about will. It's about heart, and that's how we were able to overcome this great challenge," said Gobert, who finished with 10 points, 13 rebounds and eight assists.</p>
<p>Minnesota, seeded sixth in the West, will face second-seeded San Antonio beginning Monday in the second round. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Spurs are 14.5 point favorites in Game 1</a>.</p></description>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/McDaniels_Jaden_min_231129.jpg" />
   </item>

      <description><p>The New York Knicks defeated the Atlanta Hawks 140-89 in Game 6 to win the first-round series 4-2.</p>
<p>The Knicks set an NBA record by going up 47 points at halftime, and also set a franchise record in winning a playoff game by 51 points.</p>
<p>OG Anunoby lead the team with 29 points, with Mikal Bridges adding 24.</p>
<p>"The biggest thing is we had to change our offense," Brown said of the adjustments he made throughout the series. "Again, not as drastic as we probably did halfway through the year, but we had to change it, because every possession was a grind those first three games, especially down the stretch."</p>
<p>Karl-Anthony Towns, who was put into a facilitator role, had a triple-double with 12 points, 11 rebounds, and 10 assists.</p>
<p>Jalen Brunson added that the offense required each player to sacrifice.</p>
<p>"We've always told each other that obviously we want to win, and that's most important," Brunson said. "We've said it a lot. When it comes to decisions like this, it's about actions."</p>
<p>The Knicks play the winner of the Celtics-Sixers series, with Game 7 in Boston on Saturday.</p></description>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285393/Moussa-Diabate-Wins-2025-26-NBA-Hustle-Award</link>
      <description><p>Charlotte Hornets center Moussa Diabate has won the 2025-26 NBA Hustle Award. The honor recognizes players whose effort-based contributions drive winning but rarely appear in traditional statistical categories.</p>
<p>Diabate's impact coincided directly with Charlotte's dramatic midseason turnaround. The Hornets went 9-20 through Dec. 22 before posting a 35-18 record the rest of the way, the sixth-best winning percentage in the league during that stretch. Dec. 23 marked Diabate's first game as a full-time starter.</p>
<p>The award is determined by a statistical formula tracking deflections, loose balls recovered, charges drawn, screen assists, contested shots, and box outs. Among the 124 eligible players, Diabate ranked first in offensive box outs per minute and fourth in screen assists per minute.</p>
<p>Diabate posted career-best numbers across the board in his fourth NBA season, averaging 7.9 points on 63.1 percent shooting, 8.7 rebounds, 1.9 assists, 1.0 blocks and 0.8 steals in 26.0 minutes per game. His 20 double-doubles nearly tripled his previous career total.</p>
<p>Dyson Daniels, Draymond Green, Cedric Coward and Josh Hart rounded out the top-5.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>NBA Urges Federal Regulator To Tighten Prediction Market Rules To Protect Game Integrity</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285391/NBA-Urges-Federal-Regulator-To-Tighten-Prediction-Market-Rules-To-Protect-Game-Integrity</link>
      <description><p>The NBA formally asked the Commodity Futures Trading Commission on Thursday to impose stricter regulations on prediction markets, citing risks to competitive integrity and public confidence in professional sports. The league's submission arrived on the final day of the CFTC's public comment period, which drew nearly 1,500 responses since opening March 13.</p>
<p>Dan Spillane, the NBA's executive vice president and assistant general counsel, authored the letter. He argued that prediction markets pose integrity concerns comparable to traditional sports betting, particularly when players, game officials, or team staff can trade contracts tied to competitions in which they participate.</p>
<p>"There is no higher priority for the NBA than protecting the integrity of our games and preserving public confidence in our league and in our sport," Spillane wrote.</p>
<p>The NBA's position stands apart from several peer leagues. MLB, the NHL, and the UFC have each signed partnership agreements with prediction market platforms rather than seeking tighter federal oversight.</p>
<p>Spillane flagged specific market categories as vulnerable to manipulation, including those tied to officiating decisions, player injuries, and fan conduct. The letter also called for a minimum trading age of 21, stricter than the current 18-and-older threshold used by platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket.</p>
<p>The league stopped short of taking sides in the ongoing jurisdictional dispute between individual states and the CFTC over regulatory authority.</p>
<p>Additional requests included improved data sharing between prediction market operators and leagues, along with stronger cooperation on suspicious trading activity and internal league investigations.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 30 Apr 2026 21:34:13 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285391/NBA-Urges-Federal-Regulator-To-Tighten-Prediction-Market-Rules-To-Protect-Game-Integrity</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_240515.jpg" />
   </item>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285385/Rockets-Avoid-Elimination-Again-Force-Game-6-Against-Lakers</link>
      <description><p>The Houston Rockets avoided elimination Wednesday night, defeating the Los Angeles Lakers 99-93 in Game 5 to cut the first-round series deficit to 3-2. Houston becomes the 16th NBA team to force a Game 6 after falling behind 3-0.</p>
<p>Reed Sheppard delivered key plays in the closing minutes. With Houston ahead by three and under three minutes remaining, he hit a midrange jumper and then stripped LeBron James for a dunk, pushing the lead to seven.</p>
<p>"We definitely remember what happened in Game 3, and we didn't want to let that happen again," said Sheppard, who posted 12 points and six assists while battling illness.</p>
<p>Jabari Smith Jr. led Houston with 22 points and seven rebounds. A day before the game, with the Rockets down 3-1, he told reporters, "We're obviously the better team, I feel like." Smith backed up that claim on the court Wednesday.</p>
<p>"You can't just say it and then come out and tuck your tail. You got to get out there and fight," Smith said.</p>
<p>The victory is notable given that Kevin Durant has missed four of five games due to injury. Houston has outscored Los Angeles 514-509 across the series.</p>
<p>James paced the Lakers with 25 points and seven assists in defeat. Los Angeles committed 15 turnovers, four more than Houston.</p>
<p>"Some of [our mistakes] were game-plan discipline, and some of them were just unforced errors," James said. "So, we got to be better in both areas."</p>
<p>Ime Udoka credited team development after his club's late-game collapse in Game 3 prompted sharp public criticism. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 6 is set for Friday in Houston with the Rockets favored by 3.5 points</a>.    <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant To Miss Game 6 Against Lakers With Ankle Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285381/Kevin-Durant-To-Miss-Game-6-Against-Lakers-With-Ankle-Injury</link>
      <description><p>Houston Rockets forward Kevin Durant will remain sidelined for Game 6 of his team's first-round playoff series against the Los Angeles Lakers with an ankle injury. The Rockets forced Game 6 in Houston after falling behind 3-0 in the series.</p>
<p>Durant has played in only Game 2 of the series. He missed Game 1 after banging knees with a teammate in practice. In Game 2, the veteran forward injured his ankle and has missed the last three games with a bone bruise.</p>
<p>Durant's ankle injury typically requires a minimum of two weeks out before returning to play.</p>
<p>Durant averaged 26.0 points, 5.5 rebounds and 4.8 assists this past season while finishing second in the NBA in minutes with 2,840.</p></description>
